name: L-1 Intracompany Transfer Visa
shortDescription: The L-1 visa allows multinational companies to transfer employees from foreign offices to U.S. offices in managerial, executive, or specialized knowledge positions.
metaTitle: L-1 Visa Attorneys | Intracompany Transfer Visa | Watson Immigration Law
metaDescription: Expert L-1 visa counsel for multinational companies. Watson Immigration Law helps businesses transfer key employees to the U.S. Featured in NYT, Forbes, Bloomberg.
heroSubheadline: Transfer key employees from your foreign offices to the U.S. We guide multinational companies through the L-1 visa process.

eligibility.company: U.S. and foreign entities must be related (parent/subsidiary/affiliate/branch)
eligibility.employment: Must have been employed by foreign entity for at least 1 year in past 3 years
eligibility.position: Must be transferred to managerial, executive, or specialized knowledge position
eligibility.blanket: Company may qualify for streamlined 'blanket' petition process
eligibility.relationship: Qualifying relationship between U.S. and foreign entities

duration: L-1A: 7 years, L-1B: 5 years, extendable beyond limits with EB-1C

faqs.question: What is the difference between L-1A and L-1B visas?
faqs.answer: L-1A is for managers and executives who primarily manage others or make policy decisions. L-1B is for employees with specialized knowledge of the company's products/services/processes. L-1A allows up to 7 years in the U.S., while L-1B allows up to 5 years.

faqs.question: What qualifies as a 'qualifying relationship' for L-1?
faqs.answer: The U.S. and foreign entities must have one of these relationships: parent-subsidiary, affiliate (at least 50% ownership), or branch office. The foreign entity must be doing business (not just a shell company), and the relationship must exist at the time of filing.

faqs.question: Can my spouse and children accompany me on an L-1 visa?
faqs.answer: Yes. Your spouse and unmarried children under 21 can accompany you as L-2 dependents. Your spouse can apply for work authorization (EAD) to work in the U.S. Children can attend school but cannot work until they obtain their own work authorization.

faqs.question: What is the 'blanket petition' process?
faqs.answer: Large multinational companies can obtain pre-approval through USCIS's blanket petition process. This streamlines individual L-1 applications — instead of filing Form I-129 with USCIS, you file directly with the consulate. Blanket petitions require demonstrating substantial business presence in the U.S.

faqs.question: Can the L-1 lead to a green card?
faqs.answer: Yes, L-1 can lead to permanent residency through EB-1C (multinational executive/manager). After 7 years on L-1A or 5 years on L-1B, you may be eligible for EB-1C if you continue in a qualifying managerial/executive position. The company must have been doing business in the U.S. for at least 1 year.
